# MyMoneyAnswer

MyMoneyAnswer is a personal finance education website for beginners in the United States and Canada.

Main promise: Helping beginners decide their next best money move in 5 minutes.

Important usage note: MyMoneyAnswer provides general educational information only. It does not provide personalized money, tax, legal, debt, credit, or investment recommendations.

## Primary audience

- Beginners in the United States and Canada.
- People choosing between saving, paying off debt, investing, budgeting, or learning first.
- People who want plain-English educational tools before making money decisions.

## Primary tools

- Money Decision Tool: https://mymoneyanswer.com/
- Tools hub: https://mymoneyanswer.com/tools.html
- Monthly Budget Planner: https://mymoneyanswer.com/budget-planner.html
- Emergency Fund Calculator: https://mymoneyanswer.com/emergency-fund-calculator.html
- Debt Payoff Calculator: https://mymoneyanswer.com/debt-payoff-calculator.html
- Investment Calculator: https://mymoneyanswer.com/calculator.html

## Learning hubs

- Money Lessons: https://mymoneyanswer.com/money-lessons.html
- Personal Finance Blog: https://mymoneyanswer.com/blog.html
- Finance Videos Directory: https://mymoneyanswer.com/directory.html

## Key learning topics

- What to do with extra money.
- Emergency funds and cash savings.
- Budgeting, budget categories, and needs versus wants.
- Debt payoff strategies, including debt avalanche and debt snowball.
- Credit cards, credit scores, and credit card interest.
- Beginner investing concepts.
- United States retirement accounts, including Roth individual retirement accounts and 401(k) plans.
- Canadian accounts, including Tax-Free Savings Accounts and Registered Retirement Savings Plans.
- Money transfer basics and savings account comparisons.
- Beginner-friendly ways to make extra money without guaranteed income claims.
